location.office_time_required: within commuting distance of LA, SF, or NYC hubs

level_signals: 4-5 years of experience working in a fast-paced organization
Owns compensation model for incentive-based employees
Individual contributor role building sales compensation models

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 4-5 years of experience working in a fast-paced organization, and a track record of working quickly, iteratively, and cross-functionally
You have prior experience working with sales organizations and on sales compensation models
You have business acumen, are deeply analytical, detail-oriented, and comfortable understanding and manipulating data to take action on business insights
Your deep analytical experience includes using SQL and dashboarding tools as part of your day to day work
Use forecasting, in-depth analyses, and input from RevOps, Sales leadership and Finance to build sales compensation models and inform OKR-setting
Drive the sales planning cycle by setting and tracking quota targets across various Sales teams that align with growth strategy and goals
Build dashboarding for compensation tracking and payouts that informs improvement of the sales commission model each quarter
Team members in this role are required to be within commuting distance of our Los Angeles, San Francisco, or New York City hubs.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Proficiency in Sigma, Hex and Salesforce
Experience at a high-growth startup, marketplace, or creator-focused platform
Passion for creator communities and/or new product categories that are uniquely suited to live ecommerce
Experience buying and selling in online marketplaces

scope.surface: Sales compensation modeling and commission operations for incentive-based employees across Sales teams
primary_metrics_inferred: Accuracy and timeliness of commission payouts
Quota attainment rates across sales teams
Standardization and automation of compensation processes
OKR alignment with sales growth strategy
core_responsibilities: Use forecasting, in-depth analyses, and input from RevOps, Sales leadership and Finance to build sales compensation models and inform OKR-setting
Serve as a trusted partner to Sales Leadership and People Team in rolling out new sales plans/quotas quarterly
Drive the sales planning cycle by setting and tracking quota targets across various Sales teams
Build net new compensation structures for new partnerships and customer segments
Build dashboarding for compensation tracking and payouts
Collaborate with Finance to facilitate accurate and timely commission payouts
Drive standardization and automation of all processes related to commission-setting and tracking
Support broader RevOps needs such as conducting strategic analyses, building performance monitoring, and taking on special projects

green_flags: Clear ownership of a defined domain (sales compensation and commissions)
Named specific tools and technologies expected (SQL, Sigma, Hex, Salesforce)
Explicit hub location requirements stated clearly
Company named #1 Best Startup Employer in America by Forbes — strong brand signal
Comprehensive benefits package including 401k match, parental leave, wellness allowances
Remote co-located model with flexibility across multiple hubs
red_flags: Visa sponsorship and work authorization requirements not stated
JD says 'remote co-located team' in intro but body requires commuting distance to LA, SF, or NYC — potential tension in messaging
Equity not mentioned despite startup stage
Years range stated as '4-5 years' which is unusually narrow and may be a soft ceiling

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from years range and scope of individual ownership)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
compensation.equity_mentioned set to false (not referenced in JD body)
location.work_auth and location.visa_sponsorship set to unclear (not addressed in JD)
ambiguities: The JD opens by describing Whatnot as 'a remote co-located team' but the body explicitly requires candidates to be 'within commuting distance of our Los Angeles, San Francisco, or New York City hubs.' Treated as hybrid (office presence expected) rather than remote.
Years of experience stated as '4-5 years' — ambiguous whether this is a hard floor of 4 or a target band; classified as hard with minimum_value 4.
Nice-to-have section header is '🙏 Nice to Have' which clearly signals soft requirements; all items classified accordingly.
